payload.title: Follow-up observations from Staccato Site absorption and .narada migration
payload.summary: The Staccato Site absorption and subsequent migration of the client-service Site into .narada exposed several adjacent product and doctrine gaps: lifecycle preflight was remembered after manual edits; absorption edge schema is ad hoc; workspace_root and site_root can differ; client artifact admission needs machinery; reciprocal Site references are manually fragile; and AGENTS.md must surface non-obvious containment and authority boundaries.
evidence: During the Staccato operation, we first materialized absorbed_sites and absorbed_by manually, then discovered and ran Narada sites lifecycle preflight absorb. The service Site was then moved from the client workspace root into a .narada subfolder, requiring manual edits to service, data, and ELT configs plus AGENTS/README updates. The parent folder remained a Git repo tracking only the .narada governance layer while ignoring client artifacts such as agreements and PBIX files.
proposal: 1. Make lifecycle transform workflows start from intent/preflight/plan/apply/read-back so agents check existing Narada machinery before manual projection. 2. Standardize absorption edge schema, including source, target, authority retained, admitted material, evidence, and lineage event. 3. Explicitly support workspace_root not equal to site_root in Site config, doctor, path resolution, and bootstrap guidance. 4. Add client artifact admission records for paths, classification, reason, handling policy, tracking/copy/reference posture, and retention. 5. Add reciprocal relation validation/update tooling for Site relation changes. 6. Require AGENTS.md to name workspace_root, site_root, authority locus, and forbidden collapses whenever containment is non-obvious.
payload.recommendation: Treat these as one coherent product cluster under Site lifecycle and inhabited workspace support. The immediate priority remains executing absorb machinery, but workspace_root/site_root support and reciprocal-edge validation should be considered part of making absorb safe in real client workspaces.

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
